entries.value: Gross margin = (revenue - COGS) / revenue * 100. Target >50% for service businesses, >30% for product businesses. Declining gross margin signals rising input costs or pricing pressure. Review monthly by category.

Net profit margin = net income / total revenue * 100. Target >10% for healthy SMBs. Below 5% is fragile — one bad month can cause a loss. Separate operating expenses from one-time costs for accurate trending.

Cash flow runway = current cash balance / monthly burn rate. Target >6 months for SMBs, >12 months during uncertain periods. Calculate burn rate as average of last 3 months' net cash outflow. Runway under 3 months is critical.

AR days = (accounts receivable / annual revenue) * 365. Target <30 days. Above 45 days indicates collection problems. Track aging buckets: current, 1-30, 31-60, 61-90, 90+ days overdue. Follow up aggressively at 30 days.

Budget variance = (actual - budgeted) / budgeted * 100. Target <5% absolute variance. Positive variance (overspend) above 10% requires immediate review. Track both favorable (underspend) and unfavorable (overspend) separately.

Revenue growth rate = (current_period - prior_period) / prior_period * 100. Target >10% year-over-year. Compare month-over-month for seasonality and year-over-year for trend. Negative growth for 2+ consecutive quarters requires strategic review.

Trigger an alert when any invoice is overdue by >15 days. Escalate to manager at 30 days overdue. At 60 days, flag for collections review. At 90 days, consider write-off or collections agency. Send automated payment reminders at 7, 14, and 28 days overdue.
Alert when a budget reaches 80% of allocation before 80% of the period has elapsed. Critical alert at 100% spend. Never allow auto-overspend — require explicit budget increase approval.

Send invoices within 24 hours of service delivery. Use Net 30 terms as default. Offer 2% discount for payment within 10 days (2/10 Net 30). Always include clear payment instructions and a unique invoice number. Automate recurring invoices.

Burn rate is the rate at which a company spends cash reserves above revenue. Gross burn = total monthly expenses. Net burn = total expenses - total revenue. A profitable company has negative net burn (positive cash flow).
